tool_name: Screw Conveyor Capacity, Drive Torque & Motor Power Selection

inputs.name: Bulk Density of Material (ρ_bulk)
inputs.note: Wheat ≈ 770; cement ≈ 1200; sand ≈ 1500; fly ash ≈ 700 kg/m³

inputs.name: Screw Outside Diameter (D_s)
inputs.note: CEMA 350 standard sizes; pitch = D_s for standard full-pitch screw

inputs.name: Screw Rotational Speed (n)
inputs.note: CEMA max speed: 300 mm screw → 60 rpm; 500 mm → 40 rpm for abrasive materials

inputs.name: Material Flowability / Friction Factor (F_m)
inputs.note: CEMA 350 Table 3-3 — material factor based on flowability class

inputs.name: Trough Loading (% of screw cross-section)
inputs.note: CEMA 350 Table 2-1 — maximum trough loading by material class

formulas: A_screw_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(screw_diameter_mm / 1000, 2) * trough_loading_pct / 100; Q_theory_m3h = A_screw_m2 * (screw_diameter_mm / 1000) * screw_speed_rpm * 60   // [m², m³/h] Screw Flight Area & Theoretical Capacity Reference: CEMA 350 §3.3 — Q = A_s × pitch × n × 60; standard pitch = D_s

Q_actual_m3h = capacity_t_h / (bulk_density_kg_m3 / 1000); capacity_ratio = Q_actual_m3h / Q_theory_m3h   // [m³/h, dimensionless] Actual Capacity & Capacity Check Reference: Q_vol = Q_mass / ρ_bulk; capacity_ratio ≤ 1.0 required

alpha_rad = inclination_deg * Math.PI / 180; Ci = (inclination_deg === 0) ? 1.0 : (inclination_deg <= 15) ? 1 - inclination_deg * 0.02 : (inclination_deg <= 25) ? 0.7 - (inclination_deg - 15) * 0.025 : 0.45   // [dimensionless] Inclination Capacity Reduction Factor (C_i) Reference: CEMA 350 Table 2-3 — incline factor: 0°=1.0; 15°≈0.70; 25°≈0.45; 45°≈0.25

W_material_kn_m = capacity_t_h * 9.81 * conveyor_length_m / 3600 / screw_speed_rpm * 60 / (2 * Math.PI); T_friction_knm = 0.055 * Math.pow(screw_diameter_mm / 1000, 3) * bulk_density_kg_m3 * conveyor_length_m * material_factor_Fm / 1000; T_drive_knm = W_material_kn_m + T_friction_knm   // [kN·m] Drive Torque — Material & Friction Components (T_drive) Reference: CEMA 350 §4 — drive torque = material torque + friction torque; HP method

P_material_kw = capacity_t_h * 9.81 * conveyor_length_m * Math.sin(inclination_deg * Math.PI / 180) / 3600; P_friction_kw = material_factor_Fm * bulk_density_kg_m3 * Q_actual_m3h / 3600 * conveyor_length_m * 0.055; P_motor_kw = (P_material_kw + P_friction_kw) / 0.88   // [kW] Required Motor Power (P_motor) Reference: CEMA 350 §4.4 — total HP = (HPF + HPM + HPC) / drive efficiency (η=0.88 typical)

v_tip_m_s = Math.PI * screw_diameter_mm / 1000 * screw_speed_rpm / 60   // [m/s] Peripheral Speed & Abrasion Check Reference: CEMA 350 §3.6 — tip speed limit: ≤ 1.0 m/s for abrasive materials; ≤ 1.5 m/s for non-abrasive

V1.error_msg: Required capacity exceeds screw throughput even with inclination correction — increase screw diameter, speed, or add second unit

V2.error_msg: Tip speed > 1.5 m/s: flight and trough wear rate increases exponentially — reduce speed or select harder material (AR400 flights)

V3.error_msg: Steep incline with sluggish material: significant backflow risk — evaluate vertical screw or bucket elevator alternative

smart_warnings.message: High tip speed on sluggish material: rapid liner and flight wear — reduce to < 1.0 m/s per CEMA recommendation for abrasive/sluggish class
45% trough loading on non-free-flowing material: plugging risk — reduce to 30% and increase screw diameter to maintain capacity
Long screw conveyor (> 40 m): intermediate hanger bearing required — shaft deflection and torque accumulation must be checked per CEMA §5
Inclination > 30°: CEMA capacity factors fall to < 40% — consider vertical screw conveyor (IBC) or pneumatic conveying system
High power on small screw: shaft and coupling stress high — verify shaft diameter torque rating per CEMA §6; consider upsize to 350 mm screw
